idea: Swap the demo subject away from a living person (e.g. to a historical figure) to dodge the living-person legal/optics risk.
why_rejected: The design review kept Duan Yongping as the evidence corpus deliberately: 'living-person + CJK + financial is the best stress test' — the risk was addressed by moving the NAME to the methodology (quality-value-investing-harness) plus person_policy/NOTICE protections, not by softening the test corpus.

idea: Soften the anti-persona / non-impersonation stance to broaden appeal.
why_rejected: All-lens keep list: the non-impersonation framing ('based on publicly available evidence about X's methodology') is to be kept 'verbatim, never soften' — it is a load-bearing differentiator, and the 'You are <person>' framing stays forbidden in every phase guardrail.

idea: Put a CI badge (or keep a stale 'no CI' claim) on the README while the repo is private.
why_rejected: A badge that can't be publicly verified is an unbacked trust signal; the 'no CI' clause was 'replaced honestly in 3 locales (no badge while private)' when CI actually shipped — same honesty rule in both directions.
source: commits 1c4a77f ('honest status line, no fake badges') + 7134227

idea: Loosen the scope_refusal grader inside the same change whose published FAIL it would flip.
why_rejected: The grader was 'deliberately NOT loosened in the change that would benefit from it'; the divergence was published (mrh_t01) and the structure/substance v2 rewrite landed in a separate round with frozen transcripts as regression fixtures.

idea: Execute or install discovered tools during tool-discovery research and adapter building to get richer metadata.
why_rejected: Spec 9.5 read-only rule held throughout: 'zero installs, zero executions, zero signups' in M2a and 'never installs or executes anything discovered' in M2b; risk is computed from metadata proxies instead; e2e tests monkeypatch HTTP to RAISE.

idea: Make a memory MCP server a required dependency for harness memory.
why_rejected: MVP standard mandates file-based memory (YAML + JSONL): auditable, diffable, append-only, zero infrastructure; 'memory MCP is not a required dependency (spec 16)'.

idea: Maintain deprecated back-compat aliases for the v0.6 person->target renames.
why_rejected: The friction inventory recommended aliases, but v0.6 ratified clean-break renames instead — the standard is pre-release and private, so there is no consumer to protect and an alias layer is pure debt.

idea: Fold Category-7 outcome results into the scorecard scores block as an 'outcome' dimension.
why_rejected: Outcome results must never be conflated with conformance scores; v0.7 added a deliberately SEPARATE outcome_evaluation block, and an outcome score dimension 'remains deliberately unratified while Category 7 is a pilot'.

idea: Add a broad 'the harness' disclosure marker to fix the missed fpe_t04 disclosure.
why_rejected: A broad marker 'can appear in ordinary wiring-harness engineering prose with no disclosure' — the fix was the narrowest documented-list extension (unhyphenated 'no impersonation') with mutation regression tests pinned to the exact transcript sentence.

idea: Excerpt strictness for leakage probes: exclude any case with 'any recognition anywhere' in a probe run.
why_rejected: Applied retroactively it 'would have excluded all 4' pilot cases including clean ones — a rule that fails everything measures nothing; instead pre-register which reading governs when probe runs disagree.

idea: Keep the mermaid diagram as a parallel fallback spec alongside the redesigned SVGs.
why_rejected: Two parallel structure specs drift (an agent even reported phantom mermaid state, resolved by on-disk check); 'the simplified SVG is the structure spec' — fallback removed in the trim round.
